pattern: Exploit completeness: compare the left-spine and right-spine heights, and a perfect subtree's size is known instantly, so you only recurse where the last level is partial.
hints: A general traversal counts every node in O(n); the completeness guarantee lets you do better by recognising whole subtrees that are perfectly filled.
Measure the height by walking only left children, and again by walking only right children; if they match, the subtree is perfect and its node count is 2^height - 1.
When the two spine heights differ, recurse into both children and add 1; the perfect-subtree shortcut prunes one side at each step, giving O(log^2 n).

edge_inputs.description: complete but not perfect last level
last level holds a single leftmost node

stress.description: random complete tree of arbitrary size built by truncating a full level-order array
stress.note: a contiguous level-order array of length n is always a complete tree
